nodes.name: A1 — Minority 2026 MAD = 4,707 (48% wider than majority)
A1 — Minority has 5 EDs above +15% from mean (majority has 0)
A1 — All §5.1 verdicts identical under 2021 census / 2024 OSI / 2025 TBF bases
A2 — Majority Calgary Zone A-vs-B gap = +0.36%
A2 — Minority Calgary Zone A-vs-B gap = +12.20%
A2 — Minority Calgary gap 7.71% under 2023-winner-based rule (G4 robustness)
A2b — Minority rest-of-province mean 50,336 (3.9% lower than majority)
A3 — All 6 s.15(2) invocations pass the 3-of-5 statutory threshold under corrected thresholds
Engineered-boundary signature: RMH-Banff Park (park extension chosen over populated alternatives)
B2 — Minority-majority EG asymmetry = −1.42 pp at central weight (crosswalk reading)
B2 — Sensitivity range 0.49–1.50 pp across urban weights 0.60-0.90
Canadian redistribution base-rate — Alberta 2025-26 at 67th percentile of n=6 comparator
B4 — Majority 2026 NDP seats at 50/50 uniform swing = 44
B6 — Declination shows minority is least UCP-favourable (disagrees with B2/B3/B4)
B2 — 95% Monte Carlo CI [−3.04, +0.76] pp (crosses zero); 90.5% same sign
B2 — Direction flips to +0.75 pp under 2019 votes; near-zero under 2015 votes
B-family — 1-seat gap direction is state-dependent (UCP-landslide flips it)
B — Byelection coverage too sparse + candidate-specific to enter RT3 (6 of 87 EDs)
Chen-Rodden direction prediction validates for Alberta (2019 baseline at centre of neutral distribution)
Chen-Rodden mechanism prediction fails: UCP is the more-packed party (15.9% vs 9.3% surplus)
Chen-Rodden pairwise decomposition — minority-vs-majority gap is 100% drawing, 0% geography
Absolute Chen-Rodden EG drawing components: 2019 −4.12, Majority −3.81, Minority +0.34 pp
Minority adds +5.75 pp NDP responsiveness at 50/50 via asymmetric drawing
1.5 pp uniform swing flips 6 seats toward UCP + 4 toward NDP on 2023 results
14 of 87 ridings within 3 pp two-party margin in 2023
Crosswalk reading — minority-majority asymmetry = −1.42 pp (minority more UCP-favourable)
High-resolution spatial reading — asymmetry = +4.15 pp (minority more NDP-favourable)
MAUP-v1 −1.12 pp → +3.35 pp under topology cleanup (artefact exposed)
Topology cleanup — 2,754 km² majority / 16,734 km² minority inter-ED overlap (96 pairs)
DPG perturbation flat-±500m 90% CI [+1.69, +7.67] pp (200/200 positive)
DPG perturbation tier-aware 90% CI [+2.76, +7.62] pp (200/200 positive)
DPG perturbation tight tier-aware 90% CI [+2.89, +6.48] pp
v0_5 DA-anchored MAUP rerun — sign flip to −3.64 pp (DPG-construction dominant)
Commission-map DPI ceiling — native 300-388 DPI is bottleneck, not scan resolution
Core-vs-Margin insulation test — disagreement is systematic, not a swing-VA artefact
Packing signature detected — Minority Calgary Zone A (P1/P2/P3 pass)
Packing signature NOT detected on majority Calgary (P1 fails)
Cracking signature detected — Minority Airdrie 4-way split (C1/C2/C3 pass)
Cochrane cracking-adjacent: C1/C2 pass, C3 fails (pattern but not formal signature)
Three formal signatures, all concentrated in minority map (majority detects zero)
Minority 2026 at p95.35 on mean-median (UCP-favoured tail, ESS-downgraded)
Minority 2026 at p1.6 on declination (NDP-favoured tail)
Ensemble median mean-median = −0.019, seats-at-50/50 = 0.448 — structural UCP floor
Multi-chain R-hat < 1.01 on 3/4 metrics, 1.0099 on mean-median (strict publication-grade)
Tail claims downgraded — ESS ~150 per metric bounds p100/p1.6 to p95.35/p2.5
10k-era 2019 mean-median + Majority seats-at-50/50 flags retracted under full-coverage rescore
Pre-registered checklist — minority has 3 signatures + MCMC outlier; majority has 0
Neither 2026 map crosses the sure-sign-gerrymander bar
Symmetry counter-test 1 — Edmonton zone gap (+2.0 pp maj / +1.4 pp min) << Calgary 12.2 pp
Symmetry counter-test 2 — Lethbridge + Red Deer 4-way splits found in minority, 2-way in majority
RT2 — B2/B3/B4 agree on direction; B6 declination opposes (mixed pass)
RT7 — MCMC outlier flag-pass on minority only
Three visible spatial anomalies on minority (Nolan-Hill-Cochrane, RMH-Banff Park, Olds-TH-D)
Majority Calgary hybrids — no visible anomaly (symmetric check)
CSD-level split count — null symmetric across three maps (40-66 splits each)
DA-anchoring extension — combined 79.6% majority vs 16.5% minority (5.1× asymmetry preserved)
Shared-schools community-of-interest claim refuted (R5 + R11); 20 of 21 minority hybrids cross school-division boundaries
Cochrane commuter-tie — partial support (35.8% commute to Calgary CY; sub-neighbourhood not testable)
Commission tabled divided final report (3-2, chair + opposition-nominated majority)
Motion 19 passed 44-36 on April 16, 2026 — Select Committee established, no public hearings on draft
R5 is chair Miller's personal recommendation, not commission-majority collective
April 16 action is most government-controlled among 3 Canadian comparator cases
Chair's 'no public support' Appendix C claim partially refuted (RMH-Banff, ODH, Chestermere)
Chair claim stands for Airdrie 4-way + Nolan-Hill-Cochrane (no public support)
Reference re Saskatchewan effective-representation frame set out but not adjudicated
Synthesis — six independent dimensions point same direction (minority more UCP-favorable)
